REMCHO, JOHANSEN & PURCELL
ATTORNEYS AT L.{W
220 MONTGOMERY STREET, SUITE 8OO

sacramento, california

Dear !1r. I{aI f ace:
The purpose of this letter is to request written
confirmation of advice you gave ne by telephone yesterday
regarding the duties of rny clients, Speaker Willie L. Brown,
Jr. and the Willie Brown for Assembly 1990 committee ("the
committeett). The committeets address is 1388 Sutter Street,
Suite 820, San Francisco, cA 94109. This constitutes a
request for written advice pursuant to covernment Code
section 13114 (b) .
The Conmittee is holding events in Washington, D.c.
over the course of three days in mid-April, 1991, including
receptions and meals. Varj,ous members of the California
Assembly and Senate will be invited to attend free of charge,
as will other elected state officers as defined in Government
Code section 82021. In addition, private contributors that
make a contribution to the Committee in a specified minirnum
anount will receive two tickets for attendance at the events.
While in Washington, the Speaker and other nembers of the
Legis)-a'-ure wif I :neet with members of the California
congressional delegation and federal adrninistration on issues
of mutual concern.
The Committee will pay all costs of the events,
such as neeting roon rentals, flowers, entertainment, and
food. The Conmittee will also pay for the Speakerts travel
to Washington, D.C. and lodging there during the events, but
Idill not pay for travel or lodging of other elected state
officers. 
The Connittee will ful1y disclose on its carnpaign
statements aII contributions received and expenditures rnade
in conjunction with the trip and the events in washington,
D.C.


John wallace
March 20, 19 91
Page 2
Based on these facts, you advised ne that the
events qualify as fundraisingT events for a campaign cornmittee
or candidate within the meaning of FPPC Regulation
18726.8(b). Accordingly, the free adrnission, food and
entertainment provided by the Cornnittee to the elected state
officers who attend do not constitute contributions under
Government Code section 82015 or gifts under covernment code
sections A2O2a, A620l, and 89504 and Regulations 18228 and
18726.8(b). You also advised that the conmittee's
expenditure of funds for the speaker's lodging in, and travel
to and frc:n, Washington, D.c. is permissible under Government
code section 89513 (a) because the trip is directly related to
a political, 
Iegislative or governmental purpose,
Please provide written confirmation of this advice
at your earliest convenience. Thank you for your courtesy
and cooperation.
S i,ncere Iy,

sections.question: The Committee is holding events in Washington, D.C. over three days in mid-April 1991, including receptions and meals. Various California Assembly and Senate members and other elected state officers will be invited free of charge, and private contributors making specified minimum contributions will receive two tickets. The Committee will pay all event costs (rentals, food, entertainment) and the Speaker's travel and lodging, but not other officials' travel or lodging. Does this structure comply with campaign finance laws regarding contributions and gifts, and are the Speaker's travel and lodging expenditures permissible?
sections.question_synthetic: The attorney seeks written confirmation that fundraising events in Washington, D.C. offering free admission and meals to elected officials do not constitute contributions or gifts, and that the committee's payment of the Speaker's travel and lodging is permissible under Government Code section 89513(a).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney to FPPC requesting written confirmation of prior oral advice. The letter describes the facts and the telephone advice received but does not contain FPPC's formal response. No CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section exists in this document.

The Committee will pay all event costs (rentals, food, entertainment) and the Speaker's travel and lodging, but not other officials' travel or lodging. Does this structure comply with campaign finance laws regarding contributions and gifts, and are the Speaker's travel and lodging expenditures permissible?

embedding.summary: Attorney for Speaker Willie L. Brown, Jr. requests written confirmation of prior telephone advice from FPPC Legal Division regarding whether a campaign committee's D.C. fundraising events and expense payments comply with California's Political Reform Act provisions on contributions, gifts, and permissible campaign expenditures.
